issue_title: Security decorator should log client IP address
issue_body:
### Description
We use a project called Fail2Ban on most of our web services in order to block brute force attacks. Fail2Ban can be configured to scan custom application logs and look for authentication failures and block the offending IPs. Obviously in order to do this it needs the IP address to be recorded in the log.

### Expected behaviour
Logs lines look like this:

- Token info (200) for client IP 123.123.123.123
- ... User scopes (set([u'reporting:write', u'reporting:read'])) do not match the scopes necessary to call endpoint (set(['pbx.provisioning:read'])). Aborting with 403 for client IP 123.123.123.123.

### Actual behaviour
Logs lines look like this:

- Token info (200)
- ... User scopes (set([u'reporting:write', u'reporting:read'])) do not match the scopes necessary to call endpoint (set(['pbx.provisioning:read'])). Aborting with 403.

### Steps to reproduce
Set up authentication and try to authenticate with an invalid access token and then insufficient scope.

Output of the commands:
- `python --version`
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"`

Python 2.7.12
Version: 2016.0.dev1

Would you accept a pull request for me to add the IPs into the log lines with the above format with request.remote_addr? The decorator is already accessing the Flask request object, so it should be a simple change to make.

issue_title: "Reporters" or "converters" for more intelligent report generation
issue_body:
Currently the `report` action depends on users to perform all conversion and formatting of reported items. For example, if we need to report an image, or table, the user needs to know the exact markdown code to embed image or table, and a different action would be written if we are reporting using a different format, even a different markdown-processing engine with different md syntax. Note that image can have different formats and table can be a text file, an excel file, or a variable.

We could potentially provide some *reporter* mechanism to automate this process. For example, a reporter would convert excel file to markdown table, and another reporter would convert it to html table. Even if we cannot implement all of them, we can leave room for plugins.

issue_title: Option --as for magic %get and %put
issue_body:
It can be useful to have

```
%get VAR1 --from R --as var2
```

I suppose I will implement this feature if enough people request it.

issue_title: Make style does not honor `--jobs` option
issue_body: When I use make style to generate large number of files, I cannot control how many parallel threads are invoked. I see dozens of commands firing up even if I asked for only 8 jobs.

issue_title: Recursive variable exchange
issue_body:
We have several types of variable exchanges in notebook:

http://vatlab.github.io/SOS/doc/documentation/Notebook_Interface.html#%put

I'm wondering if we can make it recursive so that I can potentially have a dictionary / list of any arbitary data types in this table, or even dictionary of dictionary of these types, and exchange that cross kernels. I am asking because I often find it convenient to write all output information from a computational procedure to a dictionary (or List in R) for use in the next language.

issue_title: -v0 to disable stdout and stderr?
issue_body: Not sure if this makes sense but we could use -v option to suppress standard input and output from commands, leaving only error messages.

issue_title: implicit provides?
issue_body:
We currently use `provides` parameter to determine auxiliary steps, so 

```
[10]
output: 'a.txt'
```
would not work for command `sos run -t a.txt` because although the step generates `a.txt`, it does not `provides` this outcome. I am wondering if we can extend SoS to allow implicit `provides` for steps with very clear output (no pattern matching, no derived output from input etc). This might make SoS easier to use, but might also confuse users.

issue_title: Case Study: Large scale text classification
issue_body:
It would be useful to build a case study for large scale text classification.  This would presumably exercise preprocessing with hashing vectorizers.  It would also be interesting compare newer systems like Keras-based neural-nets against traditional bag-of-bigrams and logistic regression.

A list of possibly interesting datasets can be found in [this paper, page 5](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/5782-character-level-convolutional-networks-for-text-classification.pdf), though presumably others are around as well.

```
Table 3: Statistics of our large-scale datasets. Epoch size is the number of minibatches in one epoch
Dataset Classes Train Samples Test Samples Epoch Size
AG’s News 4 120,000 7,600 5,000
Sogou News 5 450,000 60,000 5,000
DBPedia 14 560,000 70,000 5,000
Yelp Review Polarity 2 560,000 38,000 5,000
Yelp Review Full 5 650,000 50,000 5,000
Yahoo! Answers 10 1,400,000 60,000 10,000
Amazon Review Full 5 3,000,000 650,000 30,000
Amazon Review Polarity 2 3,600,000 400,000 30,000
```

This came out of conversation with @ogrisel

### Issue Description
What is the best way to access and control (a) device(s) in a multicam setup ?

I wrote a code based on the examples in the SDK, which is supposed to take control of cameras in a multiple D415 cameras setup. "Supposed" because right now, only 1 of the cameras is selected to capture data.
Let's be clear, the problem is clearly my code and not the hardware or the OS because I'm learning as I go.

The idea is simple : I would like to write a function which will select a camera after another to capture a pointcloud for each camera (with texture and save it to disk), and ideally, another one which will select **both** cameras to capture an image for each (and save it to disk).
What I've got so far is a program doing the different captures, but not the camera selection and is far from selecting both camera simultaneously.

I reviewed the rs-multicam example, which should have helped me, but I don't understand how to select the cameras as I want. I'm also guessing that the sensor-control example is providing some information. Unfortunately, I can't provide the code I wrote (which doesn't work anyway).

Naïvely, I thought that replacing : 
`auto dev = devices[0];`
by
`auto dev = devices[1];`
in [this example](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/9437bc24c0f4ce16193f1045ceea703a8151b96d/doc/rs400/rs400_advanced_mode.md) will selected the second device but it's not working...
[EDIT]
It is working for other cameras (Platform camera) : with two D415 and a Webcam, I can select the webcam with 
`auto dev = devices[2];`
but both
`auto dev = devices[0];` and `auto dev = devices[1];`
select the same D415...

I don't really care if the cameras are synchronized or not, because I don't want to stream and I want every data to be captured independently.

Any help is appreciated !

However, there is a bigger problem now. I started writing my own example code just to get the hang of things, with only this in main: 

```
rs::context ctx;
std::cout << "Found "<< ctx.get_device_count() << " devices" << std::endl;`
```

When run with 0 or 1 camera plugged in, the result was as expected. When I had 2 cameras plugged in, the program entered an uninterruptible sleep, so I had to reboot the system. issue_title: SliceableIteratable in scikit-image?
issue_body:
@nkeim @caspervdw @tacaswell:

I'm at SciPy 2015 working next to @stefanv. He would like me to refactor scikit-image's `ImageCollection` to behave like pims objects, backed by `SliceableIterable`. After some discussion with @JDWarner, we concluded that the best way to start is to make a very light standalone package that both pims and scikit-image can depend upon, in the pattern of [cycler](https://github.com/matplotlib/cycler), [history](https://github.com/Nikea/history), and other simple utilities. This seems better than copying code and maintaining it in two places.

To be clear, I think pims has a good niche as a user-facing package, and this is not necessarily a step toward pushing pims wholly into scikit-image. It spreads the pims-like interface as a common pattern in the scipy stack.

While this would technically add a new dependency to pims, it wouldn't be a user-facing one: the user would not normally import it, and there will be never installation issues because `sliceable_iterable` depends only on core Python and `six`.

Questions:
1. Are you comfortable with this direction?
2. If so, would you be willing to be co-owners of a new `sliceable_iterable` repo? It is important to scikit-image that there be a couple people with permissions to the repo and PyPI.

issue_title: Selling trackpy on the homepage
issue_body:
trackpy has many important strengths, and we pride ourselves on the overall quality and design of the code. However, there is a subset of those strengths that one might call "killer" features — ones that are sufficiently impressive that they could grab attention and/or convince someone to switch from an already-working solution. I can think of a few possible examples:

- Running on Python (but that's hard to miss)
- Anisotropic 3D support, which will be a major improvement in v0.3
- Handling of arbitrarily long movies in many formats
- Attractive built-in plotting
- More?

When one Googles "trackpy," the top result is the GitHub page. If we start the stopwatch when someone clicks on that link, how long until they discover one or more of these features? What could we do to shorten this time?

- Should these marquee features be listed on the documentation front page? The GitHub README?
- Should one of the banner images on the documentation front page represent 3D data?
- Should there be a tutorial on anisotropic 3D features? ( @caspervdw ?)

This was prompted by a person who, after looking briefly at the documentation, had the impression that trackpy does not support 3D.

issue_title: python setup.py bdist_rpm failed if executed from a source distribution of gitdb
issue_body:
Hi,

Using the gitdb-0.6.4.tar.gz and then:
```
tar -xvf gitdb-0.6.4.tar.gz
cd gitdb-0.6.4
python setup.py bdist_rpm
```
The last command failed:

> RPM build errors:
>     File not found: /xxx/gitdb-0.6.4/build/bdist.linux-x86_64/rpm/BUILDROOT/gitdb-0.6.4-1.x86_64/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gitdb/_perf.so
> error: command 'rpmbuild' failed with exit status 1
> 

Same command from a git clone works.
The problem is that MANIFEST.in is not included in source distribution.
To correct it, please include MANIFEST.in in MANIFEST.in file.

Regards.

Here is my patch:
```
diff --git a/MANIFEST.in b/MANIFEST.in
index 597944f..b939b5d 100644
--- a/MANIFEST.in
+++ b/MANIFEST.in
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ include LICENSE
 include CHANGES
 include AUTHORS
 include README
+include MANIFEST.in
 
 include gitdb/_fun.c
 include gitdb/_delta_apply.c
```

issue_title: Encoding failures with unicode in paths in PY2
issue_body:
Having python-2 working correctly (both in *Linux* and *Windows*) with unicodes in filepaths (and process-streams, but I don't think there are processes used in this project) is increasingly difficult, e.g. when running with unicodes in `TEMPDIR` env-var, as seen in [this travis job](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/ankostis/gitdb/build/1.0.19) .

The way to deal with such issues comes from PY3, using *pep383* and the `error='surrogateescape'` error-handler when dencoding.  Unfortunately in python-2 there is no such error-handler.

Happily, the *future* projects [provides a backported implementation](http://stackoverflow.com/a/28327415/548792).

I suggest to start depending this [*future* project](http://python-future.org/), for all git-python projects; in the end, 
a lot of compatibility code can be substituted.  But note that the POV for this project is the opposite from *2to3*: you write PY3 code and you make sure it runs on PY2.

In any case, PY3 that is the future...

issue_title: ValueError exception when asking for authored_datetime
issue_body:
I am receiving a `ValueError` exception when getting the author date of a commit in Rails project. You can replicate it with the following code:

```
> from git import Repo
> repo = Repo('rails')
> commit = repo.commit('4cf94979c9f4d6683c9338d694d5eb3106a4e734')
> commit.authored_datetime
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/dspadini/Documents/pydriller/.venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/git/objects/commit.py", line 151, in authored_datetime
    return from_timestamp(self.authored_date, self.author_tz_offset)
  File "/Users/dspadini/Documents/pydriller/.venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/git/objects/util.py", line 124, in from_timestamp
    > local_dt = utc_dt.astimezone(tzoffset(tz_offset))
ValueError: offset must be a timedelta strictly between -timedelta(hours=24) and timedelta(hours=24).
```

Tracing back the error, I got that the timestamp of the commit is 1312735823, while the tz_offset is -1864800. The problem is when creating the tzoffset: I indeed printed the timedelta 
```
local_dt = utc_dt.astimezone(tzoffset(tz_offset))

class tzoffset(tzinfo):
    def __init__(self, secs_west_of_utc, name=None):
        self._offset = timedelta(seconds=-secs_west_of_utc)
        print('Timedelta is {}'.format(timedelta(seconds=-secs_west_of_utc)))
```
and it is not in the range of [-24,24] hours, but it is actually 21 days. So maybe it may be a problem of how the offset is extracted from the git, what do you think?

issue_title: Add API endpoint to expose raiden address
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Apart from noting the address down when starting raiden there is no way for a user to query for their own address while raiden is operational

## Solution

Add a REST API endpoint that would return our raiden address.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Add a REST API endpoint that would return our address. The endpoint URL should be: `/api/<version>/address` and should simply return the ethereum address with which we initialized our raiden node.
- [ ] Document the endpoint in Rest-API.rst

issue_title: transfers: Payment couldn't be completed (insufficient funds or no route to target)
issue_body:
Trying to transfer

* 1 BOKKYwei
* to: 0xfce399a738d8c9ab9817341213c5d411ea92caee
* BOKKY token contract 0x583cbbb8a8443b38abcc0c956bece47340ea1367


like this, no?
```
curl -X POST \
http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/1/transfers/0x583cbbb8a8443b38abcc0c956bece47340ea1367/0xfce399a738d8c9ab9817341213c5d411ea92caee \
-d '{"amount":1, "identifier":0}' --header 'Content-Type: application/json'
```
but the result is:
```
{"errors": "Payment couldn't be completed (insufficient funds or no route to target)."}
```

Balance is not the problem:

```
curl http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/1/address
{"our_address": "0xc62d9f5092737fa7689579e279a3a1dd5543fb9e"}
```
* [0.39999999999997667 BOKKY](https://ropsten.etherscan.io/token/0x583cbbb8a8443b38abcc0c956bece47340ea1367?a=0xc62d9f5092737fa7689579e279a3a1dd5543fb9e)
* [6.50449528 Ether](https://ropsten.etherscan.io/address/0xc62d9f5092737fa7689579e279a3a1dd5543fb9e)


So then it must be 'no route to target'. But:

```
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/1/channels | jq
[
  {
    ...
  },
  {
    "partner_address": "0xfce399a738d8c9ab9817341213c5d411ea92caee",

and in the other direction (machine B to A):

    "token_address": "0x583cbbb8a8443b38abcc0c956bece47340ea1367"
  }
]

```

so there is a channel from `0xc62d9f5092737fa7689579e279a3a1dd5543fb9e` to `0xfce399a738d8c9ab9817341213c5d411ea92caee`, and it is funded. 

Why can't I transfer?  What am I not understanding yet?

Thanks.

issue_title: benchbuild.utils.settings depends on BB_ env vars.
issue_body:
The function `setup_config` tries to find the current configuration file inside an environment variable BB_CONFIG_FILE, which is benchbuild specific. This will provide an awkward experience in custom user code.

The function `update_env` relies on the existence of various configuration settings at specific nodes levels. This will most certainly break user code.

Possible solution: Move this code to benchbuild.settings, as this is specific to benchbuild. User-Code should get a more generic version of initialization.

issue_title: weird priority interaction
issue_body:
I ran into an unexpected issue when installing binstar-server.  

1.  setup .condarc for installing our software:
```
channels:
  - https://conda.anaconda.org/t/$TOKEN/anaconda-server/
  - https://conda.anaconda.org/t/$TOKEN/binstar/
  - defaults 
```

2.  conda install binstar-server binstar-static
3.  conda install -y supervisor    # downgrades supervisord and binstar-server to a very old version
4.  conda remove supervisor     # removes binstar-server as well

Should channel priority respect already installed packages that are from a lower priority channel rather then downgrading them?  I suspect that the lower priority supervisor (3.1.3) gets installed because it is a hard dependency when asking for binstar-server.  Then when asking just for supervisor, 3.0b2 gets installed, because it is on the highest priority channel and it downgrades everything.

I don't think people want to downgrade a package in this manner most of the time.  They still need to be able to do it if they REALLY want to.  But i don't see why that would be the normal case.

@kalefranz

issue_title: Specifications in conflict after creating frozen (exported) environment from file
issue_body:
Hello,

`pip freeze` command creates a requirements file specifying all versions of all packages that can be used later with `pip install -r`. This usually works for years on my test servers without any intervention.

I thought the similar is expected from conda with its `export` and `conda create --file` workflow. 

However, recently I got `The following specifications were found to be in conflict` error after few months of successful creations of environment on a test server.

I assume that conda export and create haven't specified everything or there must be some kind of untracked changes inside packages that happened without bumping versions. I would expect this might happen with some custom not-well-maintained packages, but in my case the error happened with quite well known packages:

```
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - boost 1.60.0 py27_0
  - pyqt 4.11.4 py27_4 -> qt >=4.8.6,<5.0
  - qt 4.8.7 4
```

How can I fix this but mainly how can I avoid such issues in the future?

issue_title: epoch specification (bang !) in versions confuse conda
issue_body:
PEP440 allows to use `!` to specify epochs. This confuses some of the conda commands, because `!` can also be the first char of `!=`.

Some examples follow:

### Specific version installation fails:

    $ conda install -c msys2 m2-mintty=1!12.2.3
    Error: invalid package specification: m2-mintty=1!12.2.3

### `env export` fails if any package has a `!` in its version

    $ conda env export -n m2tty
     Error: invalid package specification: msys2::m2-mintty=1!2.2.3=1

### The regexp of `conda.cli.common.spec_from_line`  is the reason for this:

    >>> from conda.cli import common
    >>> print(common.spec_from_line('msys2::m2-mintty-1!12.2.3'))
    msys2::m2-mintty-1 !12.2.3
    >>> print(common.spec_from_line('msys2::m2-mintty-112.2.3'))
    msys2::m2-mintty-112.2.3

issue_title: conda install --force implies --no-deps
issue_body:
From `conda install -h`:
```
  -f, --force           Force install (even when package already installed),
                        implies --no-deps.
```
I don't know when it was added that `--force` also implies `--no-deps`, but this is not how it used to be.  What `conda install -f` used to do is simply install the package (with it's dependencies) but re-extract and possibly re-download the package.

issue_title: Adding a WebAssembly platform
issue_body:
The growth of [WebAssembly]( https://webassembly.org/ ) is [really quite impressive]( https://blog.mozilla.org/blog/2017/11/13/webassembly-in-browsers/ ). It wasn't that long ago when [asm.js]( http://asmjs.org/ ) was what people targeted for performant browsers apps. There has been work on [SIMD]( https://github.com/WebAssembly/simd ), [threading]( https://github.com/WebAssembly/threads ) support for WebAssembly. Now [many browsers]( https://caniuse.com/wasm ) support WebAssembly out-of-the-box.

The growth of compilers and interpreters in WebAssembly is also quite impressive. [Emscripten emits WebAssembly by default]( https://github.com/kripken/emscripten/pull/6419 ). There have been efforts to add [WebAssembly to the GNU toolchain]( https://sourceware.org/ml/binutils/2017-03/msg00044.html ). [Binaryen]( https://kripken.github.io/talks/binaryen.html#/ ) provides a nice toolchain/compiler for WebAssembly, which can used with Emscripten & LLVM or by itself. [Several language implementations]( https://github.com/appcypher/awesome-wasm-langs ) have been ported or implemented in WebAssembly.

On the user facing front, there has even been success at getting [Python with NumPy]( http://droettboom.com/blog/2018/04/04/python-in-the-browser/ ) working in WebAssembly. Not to mention [Qt has WebAssembly support]( http://blog.qt.io/blog/2018/05/22/qt-for-webassembly/ ). [AutoCAD has a web version that has been ported to WebAssembly]( http://www.keanw.com/2018/03/the-future-of-autocad.html ). All of this really demonstrates the power of this technology and the doors that it opens. Though it also raises a really big problem as well.

Namely how do we build, package, and manage the stacks of software that can and will be used in WebAssembly in the future and how can we use this powerful tool to do better science in the future? Of course Emscripten can be used to do this, but [packaging Emscripten is hard]( https://github.com/kripken/emscripten/issues/6015 ). Not to mention it relies on things like LLVM that are also hard to package. Also there are a few other toolchain choices, so what is the right one to pick? So getting a toolchain going that can be used to easily build packages is the first challenge.

The next challenge is understanding how best to package things. Does everything get "statically linked" into one huge blob or is there a good strategy for declaring dependencies? How do we specify ABI compatibility of components? How do we constrain the target architecture of a WebAssembly built package from other architectures (e.g. macOS, Linux, or Windows)? Also when it comes to the web, [size is really important]( https://hacks.mozilla.org/2018/01/shrinking-webassembly-and-javascript-code-sizes-in-emscripten/ ). So figuring out the best optimizations to [shrink package size as much as possible]( https://github.com/kripken/emscripten/issues/6904 ) is critical for success.

I don't have good answers for these questions, but it seems clear this is an important area that we should start investigating. Interested in hearing thoughts and suggestions on how we might best explore this space.

issue_title: v19 and OSX library issues.
issue_body:
When using this module on OSX one must tweak the search paths so that the dependent modules can be loaded. 

I use this environment variable to specify where to load the dylib: 
declare -x SQLANY_API_DLL="/Applications/SQLAnywhere17/System/lib64/libdbcapi_r.dylib"

However, this will still fail unless you modify the locations of the dynamic modules. 

```
jamess-mbp-2016:lib64 jamesrusso$ otool -L libdbcapi_r.dylib 
libdbcapi_r.dylib:
	libdbcapi_r.dylib (compatibility version 0.0.0, current version 0.0.0)
	**/Applications/SQLAnywhere17/System/lib64/**libdblib17_r.dylib (compatibility version 0.0.0, current version 0.0.0)
	**/Applications/SQLAnywhere17/System/lib64/**libdbtasks17_r.dylib (compatibility version 0.0.0, current version 0.0.0)
	/usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (compatibility version 1.0.0, current version 1213.0.0)
	/usr/lib/libstdc++.6.dylib (compatibility version 7.0.0, current version 104.1.0)

```
Then do the same for libdblib17_r and libdbtasks17_r

After all this, I made progress but am now getting this error...

sqlanydb.OperationalError: ('Connection error: Missing or invalid encryption DLL "/Applications/SQLAnywhere17/System/', -832)

I know its supposed to be loading libdbrsa17_r.dylib, but I don't see that as a dependency?

This all works if I put my python script in the lib64 directory. 

Any ideas?

issue_title: Static resources are always cached in RAM
issue_body:
We need a configuration knob to turn this on/off. The default should probably be off instead of on.

Related issue: https://github.com/AspenWeb/pando.py/issues/127 (if you don't cache the file in RAM you have to deal with the fact that it can be modified by another process).

issue_title: Get rid of custom alembic CLI commands
issue_body:
We can simply use:

```
from flask_alembic import alembic_script
app_manager.add_command('db', alembic_manager)
```

As per [documentation](https://flask-alembic.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)

issue_title: Django: Make m2m through table fields available on edges
issue_body:
When a `DjangoConnectionField` traverses a many-to-many field it would be nice to have the option to expose the fields of any through-table on the edges of the relationship.

[As much a note for myself as a feature request]

issue_title: Exceptions being hidden in resolve methods
issue_body:
I'm using graphene 0.7.1 and am really liking it! Thanks!

I ran into an issue that took me quite some time to figure out. Given a `DjangoNode` like this:

```
class NetworkEntityType(DjangoNode):
    class Meta:
        model = NetworkEntity
        only_fields = (
            'id',
            'type',
            'short_name',
            'full_name',
            'website',
        )

    availability = graphene.String()

    def resolve_availability(self, args, info):
        1/0
```

The availability attribute comes back as null and there is no exception reported. Is this by design or accident? 

It makes it very difficult to debug the resolve method -- I missed a typo for a long time because I had no error message to guide me.  My error wasn't a division by zero obviously, but that detail would confuse the issue. I also get the same behavior if I use `DjangoObjectType` instead of `DjangoNode`.

The `NetworkEntity` model is pretty vanilla, I can provide it if you want but I don't think there are any surprises

issue_title: AttributeError on attempt to resolve coroutine with experimental executor
issue_body:
I'd like to play with new experimental executor included with `graphql-core==1.1`, but unfortunately it didn't work well due to error below:

```
An error occurred while resolving field Query.allTeams
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/playpauseandstop/Projects/project/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphql/execution/experimental/resolver.py", line 30, in on_complete_resolver
    return result.then(on_resolve).then(__func).catch(on_error)
AttributeError: 'coroutine' object has no attribute 'then'
```

As well one more error occurred,

```
sys:1: RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'core_resolve_teams' was never awaited
```

----

My setup:

```
aiohttp==2.0.7
asyncpg==0.10.1
graphql-core==1.1
```

----

The code behind test:

**views.py**

```py
from aiohttp import web
from graphql import graphql
from graphql.execution import executor
from graphql.execution.executors.asyncio import AsyncioExecutor

from .schemas import GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA


executor.use_experimental_executor = True


async def graphql_core(request: web.Request) -> web.Response:
    query = await request.json()['query']
    result = await graphql(
        GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA,
        query,
        context_value={'db': request.app['db']},
        executor=AsyncioExecutor(loop=request.app.loop),
        return_promise=True)

    status = 400 if bool(result.errors) else 200
    return web.json_response(result, status=status)
```

**schemas.py**

```py
import graphql

from .resolvers import core_resolve_attr, core_resolve_teams


GRAPHQL_CORE_TEAM = graphql.GraphQLObjectType('Team', lambda: {
    'slug': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr()),
    'name': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr()),
    'originalName': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr('original_name'),
    ),
})


GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA = graphql.GraphQLSchema(
    query=graphql.GraphQLObjectType('Query', lambda: {
        'allTeams': graphql.GraphQLField(
            graphql.GraphQLList(GRAPHQL_CORE_TEAM),
            resolver=core_resolve_teams,
        ),
    }),
)
```

**resolvers.py**

```py
def core_resolve_attr(name: str=None) -> Callable:
    def resolver(source: Any, args: Any, context: Any, info: Any) -> Any:
        return source[name or info.field_name]
    return resolver


async def core_resolve_teams(source: Any,
                             args: Any,
                             context: Any,
                             info: Any) -> list:
    async with context['db'].acquire() as conn:
        return await conn.fetch('TABLE teams')
```

----

But when I commented (removed) `executor.use_experimental_executor = True` line everything works fine and,

```
{
    allTeams {
        slug
    }
}
```

GraphQL query executed well.

Any ideas on why the `AttributeError` error happened with experimental executor? Or this error should be reported to https://github.com/syrusakbary/promise instead?

issue_title: Is there any way to get select_related to work?
issue_body:
I can't seem to get ``select_related`` to be honored at all, in my experience.  Can anyone help me identify how it should work?

``Job`` has a foreign keys to ``Push`` and to ``FailureClassification`` models.
Here's my Graphene Schema:

    class PushGraph(DjangoObjectType):
        class Meta:
            model = Push
            filter_fields = ('revision', )
            interfaces = (graphene.relay.Node, )
    
        jobs = DjangoFilterConnectionField(JobGraph)
    
        def resolve_jobs(self, args, context, info):
            return (Job.objects
                .filter(push=self, **args)
                .select_related("failure_classification")
            )

This seems to have no effect on doing a join in the queries.  I get tons of repeat queries for each ``Job`` that's returned.  Is this a bug?  Or a result of using ``DjangoFilterConnectionField``? as mentioned in Issue 179?

Thanks!

issue_title: How to have a query which returns two different types
issue_body:
Hi! I'm trying to get a query that return one of two types which are both children of the same ancestor type; in other words, I'd like to connect polymorphic sqlalchemy models to the graphene schema. Let me explain with code:

```
# models.py
class User(Base):                                                                                         
    __tablename__ = 'users'                                                                               
    id = Column(CHAR(32), default=generate_uuid, primary_key=True, nullable=False)                        
                                                                                                          
    email = Column(Text, nullable=False)                                                                  
    password = Column(Text, nullable=False)                                                               
    type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    __mapper_args__ = {'polymorphic_identity': 'user', 'polymorphic_on': type}

class Patient(User):                                                                 
    __tablename__ = 'patients'                                                       
    id = Column(CHAR(32), ForeignKey(User.id), primary_key=True, nullable=False)     
    first_name = Column(Text, nullable=True)                                         
   __mapper_args__ = {'polymorphic_identity': 'patient'}

class Clinician(User):                                                                 
    __tablename__ = 'clinicians'                                                       
    id = Column(CHAR(32), ForeignKey(User.id), primary_key=True, nullable=False)
    code = Column(Text, nullable=True)
   __mapper_args__ = {'polymorphic_identity': 'clinician'}
```
Those are my models; using sqlalchemy, from a user I can get all the `son` fields:
```
user = db_session.query(User).first() # let's supose this first user is type 'clinician'
user.first_name # this is correct
```
I'd like to use this interface in the schema; so if the logged user is a Patient, the system returns all the fields of a patient (and its user), and if the logged user is a Clinician, the system will return all the fields of the clinician (and its user).

```
class Query(ObjectType):
    me = Field(description="description")
    
    def resolve_me(self, info):                                       
        token = info.context['request'].headers.get('Authorization')  
        return get_user(token)                 
```

I've tried with GenericScalar, SQLAlchemyConnection and Field; so far, I only get to query the fields of User, but cannot query the specific fields of the Patient or Clinician.

Could you give any hint on which approach should I take?

issue_title: How to use spectral_norm in tensorlayer?
issue_body:
### New Issue Checklist

### Issue Description
Recently, I read this paper [[The GAN Landscape: Losses, Architectures, Regularization, and Normalization]
](https://arxiv.org/abs/1807.04720) and  want to apply spectrum_norm to tensorlayer, but it doesn't matter。
I refer to this [code](https://github.com/google/compare_gan/blob/master/compare_gan/src/gans/ops.py) and change it on this basis.

### Reproducible Code


[INSERT CODE HERE]

**spectral_layer.py**
```python
import tensorflow as tf

from tensorlayer.layers.core import Layer
from tensorlayer.layers.core import LayersConfig

from tensorlayer import tl_logging as logging

def spectral_norm(input_):
  """Performs Spectral Normalization on a weight tensor."""
  if len(input_.shape) < 2:
    raise ValueError(
        "Spectral norm can only be applied to multi-dimensional tensors")
  w = tf.reshape(input_, (-1, input_.shape[-1]))
  u_var = tf.get_variable(
      input_.name.replace(":", "") + "/u_var",
      shape=(w.shape[0], 1),
      dtype=w.dtype,
      initializer=tf.random_normal_initializer(),
      trainable=False)
  u = u_var

  power_iteration_rounds = 1
  for _ in range(power_iteration_rounds):
    # `v` approximates the first right singular vector of matrix `w`.
    v = tf.nn.l2_normalize(
        tf.matmul(tf.transpose(w), u), axis=None, epsilon=1e-12)
    u = tf.nn.l2_normalize(tf.matmul(w, v), axis=None, epsilon=1e-12)

  # Update persisted approximation.
  with tf.control_dependencies([tf.assign(u_var, u, name="update_u")]):
    u = tf.identity(u)

  u = tf.stop_gradient(u)
  v = tf.stop_gradient(v)

  # Largest singular value of `w`.
  norm_value = tf.matmul(tf.matmul(tf.transpose(u), w), v)
  norm_value.shape.assert_is_fully_defined()
  norm_value.shape.assert_is_compatible_with([1, 1])
  w_normalized = w / norm_value
  w_tensor_normalized = tf.reshape(w_normalized, input_.shape)
  return w_tensor_normalized


class SpectralDenseLayer(Layer):
    def __init__(
            self,
            prev_layer,
            n_units=100,
            act=None,
            use_sn=False,
            W_init=tf.truncated_normal_initializer(stddev=0.1),
            b_init=tf.constant_initializer(value=0.0),
            W_init_args=None,
            b_init_args=None,
            name='spectraldense',
    ):
        super(SpectralDenseLayer, self
             ).__init__(prev_layer=prev_layer, act=act, W_init_args=W_init_args, b_init_args=b_init_args, name=name)

        logging.info(
            "SpectralDenseLayer  %s: %d %s, use_sn: %s" %
            (self.name, n_units, self.act.__name__ if self.act is not None else 'No Activation', use_sn)
        )
        # check layer name (fixed)
        Layer.__init__(self, prev_layer=prev_layer, name=name)

        # the input of this layer is the output of previous layer (fixed)
        self.inputs = prev_layer.outputs

        self.n_units = n_units

        if self.inputs.get_shape().ndims != 2:
            raise AssertionError("The input dimension must be rank 2, please reshape or flatten it")

        n_in = int(self.inputs.get_shape()[-1])

        with tf.variable_scope(name):
            W = tf.get_variable(
                name='W', shape=(n_in, n_units), initializer=W_init, dtype=LayersConfig.tf_dtype, **self.W_init_args
            )
            if use_sn:  # if use spectral normalization
                W = spectral_norm(W)
            else:  # we don't use spectral normalization
                pass

            self.outputs = tf.matmul(self.inputs, W)

            if b_init is not None:
                try:
                    b = tf.get_variable(
                        name='b', shape=(n_units), initializer=b_init, dtype=LayersConfig.tf_dtype, **self.b_init_args
                    )
                except Exception:  # If initializer is a constant, do not specify shape.
                    b = tf.get_variable(name='b', initializer=b_init, dtype=LayersConfig.tf_dtype, **self.b_init_args)

                self.outputs = tf.nn.bias_add(self.outputs, b, name='bias_add')

            self.outputs = self._apply_activation(self.outputs)

        self._add_layers(self.outputs)
        if b_init is not None:
            self._add_params([W, b])
        else:
            self._add_params(W)


```
And use the SpectralDenseLayer() in the **main.py** define as follows 
```
#! /usr/bin/python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import tensorflow as tf
from tensorlayer.layers import *
import tensorlayer as tl
from spectral_layer import *
tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.DEBUG)

network = tl.layers.InputLayer(x, name='input_layer')
network = tl.layers.DropoutLayer(network, keep=0.8, name='drop1')
network = SpectralDenseLayer(network, n_units=800,
                                act = tf.nn.relu, use_sn=True, name='relu1')
network = tl.layers.DropoutLayer(network, keep=0.5, name='drop2')
network = SpectralDenseLayer(network, n_units=800,
                                act = tf.nn.relu,  use_sn=True, name='relu2')
network = tl.layers.DropoutLayer(network, keep=0.5, name='drop3')
network = SpectralDenseLayer(network, n_units=10,  use_sn=True,
                                name='output_layer')

y = network.outputs
cost = tl.cost.cross_entropy(y, y_, name='cost')
train_params = network.all_params

train_op = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999,
                            epsilon=1e-08, use_locking=False).minimize(cost, var_list = train_params)

tl.layers.initialize_global_variables(sess)

issue_title: Jacaard_similarity_accuracy to the objectives
issue_body:
One of widely-used metrics for evaluationg the results of a segmentation task is the Jaccard_similarity_coefficients. It is as simple as diving the intersection region over the union region between two binary maps. 

Adding this accuracy metric to the objectives (just like binary_accuracy) can be so helpful. 
For more information, please see this Wikipedia page: 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaccard_index

### OS / Environment

Distributor ID:	ArchLinux
Description:	ArchLinux
Release:	rolling-release

### Manticore version

Last revision (4c0f4bc6cf7509eb0f3399327fb494188b137671)

### Python version

Python 2.7.13

### Summary of the problem

Loading a simple contract triggers an NotImplemented error because bytes32 type is not supported.

### Step to reproduce the behavior

This is a small solidity contract to reproduce this issue:

```
contract C {
    function f() public returns(bytes32){
        return "abc";
    }
}
```

You can trigger this exception using the manticore cli:

```
$ manticore error.sol

### Expected behavior

I expect manticore to support the bytes32 type.

### Actual behavior

issue_title: message throttling should only apply to sync messages
issue_body:
Applying message throttling to the custom messages leads to:

* errors if people don't use the right callbacks to decrement the pending message counter
* prioritizing custom messages over sync messages

I think we should use the message throttling *only* for sync messages.

To do this, I think we should have a special callback that wraps the generic callback to decrement the pending messages counter for idle status messages corresponding to sync messages.

issue_title: Output widget module
issue_body:
Right now, the browser Output widget model/view is always patched into the controls module by hand by each manager. This is awkward. The only reason we did this is so that there would be one javascript module name for the entire ipywidgets python package. However, now we don't have that anyway with the base and controls module.

How about we make a new package just for the output widget: `@jupyter-widgets/output` or something. Then each manager just has to look for that specific package and substitute its implementation in, rather than looking for the controls package and then checking the module name. This also lets us treat the controls package like any other package, and not *have* to patch something in.

issue_title: WIP Dropdown interaction spec
issue_body:
We have some problems with the dropdown releasing keyboard handling. To make things clear, here is a draft of a user interaction spec for the dropdown. The dropdown has two parts, the header and the dropdown list. The header always displays the currently-selected value.


(see [apple](https://developer.apple.com/library/content/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/OSXHIGuidelines/ControlswithMenus.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/20000957-CH100-SW1) and [microsoft](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/dn742404(v=vs.85).aspx) guidelines)

## States

### Closed, not focused
dropdown list: invisible
header: current value

* click on header moves to "Open, focused" state on button *down*
* keyboard handling (e.g., tabbing) can focus the control and move to "Closed, focused"

### Closed, focused
dropdown list is invisible, header is focused with a visible border indicating focus.

| | OS X | Windows |
---|---|---
Arrow keys | up/down open list | up/down and left/right cycle through values in the header
Open list | space | enter or space

(Mac: up/down arrow keys open the dropdown. On windows, the up/down and left/right arrow keys cycle through choices without opening the list, and the enter keydown opens the dropdown)

### Open, focused
dropdown list is visible, initially the header element is highlighted.

|Item|OSX|Windows|
---- |---|---
Dropdown position | covers the header, positioned so the selection is over the header | below the header, even if that extends beyond the window
Currently selected item | A checkmark by the item | Shows in the header
arrow keys |

issue_title: html-manager loading requirejs can mess up a page
issue_body:
The html manager's embed script loads requirejs on a page if it is not already loaded. This can really mess up a page if (a) requirejs is not loaded, and (b) the page loads other umd modules. For example, the widgets docs includes a github buttons package that is umd. If the widget embedding script loads requirejs before the github buttons package, it sees that require is defined and tries to load a module which actually causes an error. Regardless, loading requirejs as a global on a page can really mess it up.

So, what should we do?

(a) try to load requirejs only for widgets and things widgets pulls in. Don't know if this is possible.
(b) Webpack 3 apparently has some support for a new javascript dynamic `import` statement - I don't know if this would allow us to pull in URLs like we need for custom widgets.
(c) tell the page that it's responsible for loading requirejs - that way at least we make the system conform to our expectations in a way that makes sense for the system.

issue_title: ipywidgets 6 + jupyter-js-widgets 7 incompatibility
issue_body:
I ran into an issue when installing ipywidgets to a fresh conda environment and jupyter notebook loading jupyter-js-widgets/extension from `/usr/local/share/jupyter` vs. `/Users/grant/anaconda/envs/mycondaenv/share/jupyter`. ipywidgets in the conda environment was 6.0.0 and jupyter-js-widgets/extension was linked to my dev install of ipywidgets (7.0.0). 

The issue looked like:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/512354/27140829-61c01388-50db-11e7-9be1-a3587ed700e1.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/512354/27140792-4d043604-50db-11e7-922c-c832d2529db6.png)

The root of the issue is a breaking change introduced by https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/pull/1194, specifically https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/commit/9d896f76d01b23b3b9fb60a2948141f77da769ef#diff-30b37447269c75d7eff843470858ea28, which looks for widget state in `msg.content.data.state` vs `msg.content.data`. 

1. I remember in previous versions, ipywidgets would return an error message if there as a version mismatch between JS and Python? That would've made this issue much easier to debug.
2. Given that this breaks backwards-compatibility, is there a legitimate need to move widget state from `msg.content.data` to `msg.content.data.state`? This shouldn't affect anyone unless they version mismatch between JS and Python, so if we solve #1 then we solve this.
3. Is this the way that jupyter paths are supposed to work? I assumed that an nbextension installed to a conda environment would take precedence over an nbextension installed elesewhere?

issue_title: --no-latest-check and good defaults
issue_body:
So in our initial toe-dipping investigations we had discovered when running sops on hardware inside our corporate network, it would take > 30 seconds to execute, but from elsewhere it would take < 1 second. It turns out the root cause is due to us not declaring the `--no-latest-check` argument when running it, as the method it calls is not aware of common proxy environment variables (`https_proxy` and the likes) and the wait is due to proxy timeout, in which case it gives up and just completes the task anyhow.

We could just send a pull request adding proxy support to the check, however I think now is a good time to have a discussion about wether or not the idea of a security product like sops 'phoning home' on every execution by default is a good idea and that it outweighs the risks of out-of-date and potentially vulnerable software being exploited. A very large number of our machines will be automatically executing this code frequently as well as engineers and not necessarily be in an immediate position to upgrade the moment a new release is made available. At the same time we could look at other options - disabling the check everywhere or forking code being the first to mind.

Is this the right default to have?

issue_title: StreamingState allocated with new, deallocated with free
issue_body:
From looking at `deepspeech.cc`, it seems that `StreamingState` gets allocated with `new` and deallocated with `free`. This is unsound on two levels: first, free doesn't run the destructors of `StreamingState`, second if you allocated something with `new` you may not deallocate it with `free`: there can be two different allocators underneath.

Related to #1492

1) stack name (version) [instance id] - private IP

issue_body:
Hi,

Thanks for all the hardwork on putting the examples for R-CNNs; this is very cool. 

I am trying to run the Faster-R-CNN code (https://github.com/tensorpack/tensorpack/tree/master/examples/FasterRCNN) and reproduce the results. I installed all the dependencies, set all the data, and used the following command to run training for Faster-R-CNN with the mask option on.
`./train.py --gpu 4,5,6,7 --load COCO-ResNet50-MaskRCNN.npz`

After 280K steps of training, I tried to check mAP by running the following command:
`./train.py --evaluate output.json --load train_log/maskrcnn_0523/model-280000. --gpu 0,1,2,3`

which gives me the following mAP results:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39837223/40853992-ebe6de3e-659d-11e8-9522-a4c9d4398b18.png)

I noticed that this mAP results (31.6/28.6 at IoU(0.5:0.95)) are much worse than the results reported in the website (https://github.com/tensorpack/tensorpack/tree/master/examples/FasterRCNN#results), which is as follows: 
> Backbone | FASTRCNN_BATCH | resolution | schedule | mAP (bbox/segm)
> R50-C4 | 256 | (800, 1333) | 280k | 36.8/32.1
> R50-C4 | 512 | (800, 1333) | 360k | 37.8/33.1

From Tensorboard, I can see that mAP is not monotonically increasing over the steps; It's behavior is quite strange, as shown below... (I don't understand why mAP decreases over the steps until step 360K...)

Could you help me understand why I don't get the baseline mAP as reported in the website? Is there any parameters that I need to change further to reproduce the baseline results? 


Thanks for your help,
Jungwook

issue_title: Add direct option access with auto creation
issue_body:
Direct access to `_option` is important to add and update the options that are not accessible via the API. Dealing with the options dictionary directly is not productive, especially when adding options that do no exist. To make this easier, I suggest adding a function like `set_option(path, value)` to the `Chart` base class where `path` is a JSONPath or XPath-like notation. For example:
```
bar = Bar('Bar Chart')
bar.set_option('/xAxis[0]/axisTick/alignWithLabel', True)
```
If that option already exists int he options dictionary, it will update it with the new value. But if it doesn't exist, it will create any missing part/nodes of it first. For example, if `axisTick` was not created by pyecharts, the method will append `axisTick` and `alignWithLabel` to `_option[xAxis][0]` and give it the value `True`. This can make it easier to bridge the gap between pyecharts and ECharts.

issue_title: Validation error in CodeCommit Trigger
issue_body:
It appears that the validation for the CodeCommit Trigger class does not currently allow the use of any intrinsic functions for the `Events` property, and only accepts a list of hard coded values.

https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/45582eb1d21a6cc9cfa608f626d8acbf0317f37f/troposphere/codecommit.py#L18-L32

We are trying to allow for the dynamic selection of  the values with a  parameter, but encounter errors when attempting to use a `Ref`.  A snippet of our trigger definition, and the error encountered is below:

```
repo_trigger1 = codecommit.Trigger(
            Name = Ref(trigger_1_name),
            CustomData = Ref(trigger_1_custom_data),
            DestinationArn = Ref(trigger_1_destination_arn),
            Branches = Ref(trigger_1_branches),
            Events = Ref(trigger_1_events),
        )
```
We are able to successfully generate the template when changing the `Events` assignment to:

```Events = ["all"],```

I believe we just need to check if the value is one of the Helper functions before iterating through the events.  I will try to get a fix pushed up for review.

issue_title: Rapidly sending messages leads to loss of messages
issue_body:
Here is a scenario where a listener binds to a port and then multiple senders come in quick succession and send a message:
```
def test():
    port = 8876
    ctx = zmq.Context.instance()
    listener = ctx.socket(zmq.ROUTER)
    listener.setsockopt(zmq.LINGER, 1)
    listener.bind('tcp://*:{}'.format(port))
    fds = set()
    und = set()
    count = 10
    for i in range(count):
        sock = ctx.socket(zmq.DEALER)
        sock.setsockopt(zmq.LINGER, 1)
        sock.connect('tcp://127.0.0.1:{}'.format(port))
        print(sock.FD)
        fds.add(sock.FD)
        und.add(sock.underlying)
        sock.send('m:{}:{}:{}'.format(i, sock.FD, sock.underlying).encode())
    print(len(fds), len(und))
    r = 0
    while r != count:
        try:
            print(listener.recv_multipart(flags=zmq.NOBLOCK))
            r += 1
        except:
            time.sleep(.01)
            continue
    print('got all')
    print('done')
```

**Output of some runs of the above code**
All file descriptors different, still only 2 messages received

13
15
18
20
23
25
28
30
32
10 10

```
If i try to send 20 or more messages (change `count` to 20) then only one message is received, the last one. But if i introduce a sleep of even .01 seconds after a send or do any operation like appending socket to a list, the results change, i get all messages, here is an example
```
def test():

```

issue_title: Unify resource management between tasks and actors.
issue_body:
There are some inconsistencies in the way we are managing resources (CPUs and GPUs).

- Right the local scheduler ignores the dynamic resource capacity of the node when scheduling actor tasks.
- Tasks that use GPUs don't have access to `ray.get_gpu_ids()`.
- When we reserve GPUs for actors and GPUs for tasks, these things are completely disjoint from one another. For example, GPUs are reserved for tasks by local schedulers and GPUs are reserved for actors by Redis. They should both be done by the local scheduler.

issue_title: Handling `unicode` in recurly configuration
issue_body:
I was trying to create accounts from the API of Recurly using the Python client. Python 2.7, recurly 2.2.17

When creating a new account with a first name (or any other attribute actually) containing non-ascii characters (fetched as a `unicode`), saving the entity raises `UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte [...]`

A simple `account.first_name = u'Frédérique-Fançois'.encode('utf-8')` still raises the same error but at another level in the module.

For more details about the problem see this [stackoverflow post](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/33783783/recurly-python-client-raising-unicodedecodeerror-when-supplying-non-ascii-charac).

This gist reproduces the problem and explains the details of the problem: [code](https://gist.github.com/maximehardy/d3a0a6427d2b6791b3dc)

To sum up the solution is to ensure that parameters like `recurly.API_KEY` come into `httplib` headers as `str`.

I think that documentation about the issue would be a good start, but going further you could either raise an error if configuration values are `unicode` or you could force a conversion (but be careful with encoding).

issue_title: Client.port() only reports the UDP exposed port mapping if the same port is configured to be mapped on UDP and TCP.
issue_body:
E.g.:
`docker ps`
```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE         COMMAND        CREATED        STATUS        PORTS                                         NAMES
6348dd5bc7c2  ubuntu:14.04  /usr/bin/bash  8 seconds ago  Up 8 seconds  0.0.0.0:49153->80/udp, 0.0.0.0:49159->80/tcp  expose_ports
```

`In ipython`
```
In [1]: docker_client.port('6348dd5bc7c2', 80)
Out[1]: [{u'HostIp': u'0.0.0.0', u'HostPort': u'49153'}]
```

The reason seems to lie in `/docker/api/container.py`, line 208-210
```python
def port(self, container, private_port):

...

h_ports = port_settings.get(s_port + '/udp')
if h_ports is None:
    h_ports = port_settings.get(s_port + '/tcp')
```

Instead of basically only accepting a port number for `private_port` and trying for udp and tcp exposed ports, I'd suggest that the method rather accepts an explicit `private_port` input like `1234/tcp`.

issue_title: [BUG]: `getUserData` method is broken.
issue_body:
The [`getUserData` method](https://github.com/AfricasTalkingLtd/africastalking-python/blob/master/africastalking/AfricasTalkingGateway.py#L274) always returns `AfricasTalkingGatewayException`.

On further probing, it turns out the method makes a `POST` requests instead of a `GET` request to the api endpoint. This is because of how the [sendRequest](https://github.com/AfricasTalkingLtd/africastalking-python/blob/master/africastalking/AfricasTalkingGateway.py#L283) method is implemented.

How to reproduce:

```python
url = 'https://api.africastalking.com/version1/user?username=your-username'
headers = { 'Accept' : 'application/json',  'apikey' : XXXX}
r = requests.post(url, headers)
print(r.status_code) # status code: 405
print(r.test) # text: HTTP method not allowed, supported methods: GET
```
I apologize I have used `requests` to reproduce the error instead of the `AfricasTalkingGateway` class. This because the error gets masked by the `AfricasTalkingGatewayException` I'm using requests to simulate [this line](https://github.com/AfricasTalkingLtd/africastalking-python/blob/master/africastalking/AfricasTalkingGateway.py#L289) in `sendRequest`.

Changing the http method from `POST` to `GET` fixes this:
```python
url = 'https://api.africastalking.com/version1/user?username=your-username'
headers = { 'Accept' : 'application/json',  'apikey' : XXXX}
r = requests.get(url, headers)
print(r.status_code) # status code: 200
print(r.test) # text:{'UserData': {'balance': 'KES XXX'}}
```

Since it doesnt seem obvious what the right version argument should be, maybe this should default to ToneAnalyzerV3Beta.latest_version?

issue_title: Refresh Token Client Side
issue_body:
This might be somehow related to https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/issues/92 but i felt that it deserved a new issue.

While reading the official documentation on Refresh Tokens, i found this bit `Each time the user loads the page, you can check if there is an existing non-expired token and if it's close to being expired, refresh it to extend their session`. 

The thing is, i want my API to support both Web and Mobile, for my Web part i am using jQuery to ask for the refresh tokens. The thing is how can i do this part `and if it's close to being expired, refresh it to extend their session` without passing my Django secret key to the template so that i can decode the token in my local storage and figure out when it is going to expire??

If i should not decode the token, then for every page load i would have to make an additional request to ask if my current token has not expired yet? That seems a bit overkill to me and it is whats causing me to be confused about it.

What is the correct approach here??

Thanks

issue_title: don't use sum_elems to get a scalar in Node unit tests.
issue_body: A common pattern in our tests is to compute some Tensor-valued function, use sum_elems on it to reduce it to a scalar and then backprop and compare the evaluated gradient to the gradient computed on forward using finite differences. sum_elems is a bad scalar reduction because you end up with dy/d(element_i)=1 for every element. The resulting symmetry of the gradient can hide some common bug patterns (e.g., I found one hidden by this in fold_rows and circular convolution). A bit more reliable would be to reshape into a vector and do pickneglogsoftmax on some element to compute the loss- this the elements of this gradient will not be 1, so it will hide fewer bugs.

issue_title: Memory leak when passing arrays of strings as methods' arguments
issue_body:
Hello all.

I've been experience some "out of memory" errors when using pyjnius, specially when passing arrays of strings to methods. Here is an example:

Consider this sample python file:

```python
import sys
import jnius_config
jnius_config.add_options('-Xms100M', '-Xmx100M')

from jnius import autoclass

TestFactory = autoclass('test.Test')
test = TestFactory()

for c in xrange(int(sys.argv[1])):
    arg = ["some string" for _ in xrange(1000)]
    test.test(arg)
    print "counter:", c
```

And the corresponding java implementation:

```java
package test;

public class Test{
    public void test(String[] arg){}
    public void test_noarg(){}
}
```

What I've been experiencing is that after around 1600 test.test(arg) calls, the process struggles with memory, eventually crashing. This does not happen if one substitutes test.test(arg) for test.test_noarg(), what points to a problem in the handling of String arrays arguments. In fact, valgrind --leak-check=full reports that several bytes are lost at line 538 of jnius_conversion.pxi ( j_string = j_env[0].NewStringUTF(j_env, <bytes>arg)).

And if I comment-out lines 538 to 542, the problem disappears. 
```python
                # j_string = j_env[0].NewStringUTF(
                #         j_env, arg)
                #
                # j_env[0].SetObjectArrayElement(
                #         j_env, <jobjectArray>ret, i, j_string)
```

issue_title: Cython + 'unexpected keyword argument' hack
issue_body:
I'm not sure if Luigi or Cython should be blamed, but here's the problem I found. As you know, we can find this snippet in worker.py in _run_get_new_deps:
```python
try:
    task_gen = self.task.run(tracking_url_callback=self.tracking_url_callback)
except TypeError as ex:
    if 'unexpected keyword argument' not in str(ex):
        raise
```
Although it's clearly a hack, it does work most of the time. The exception message in indeed: `got an unexpected keyword argument 'tracking_url_callback`"

When we cythonize our program though, the exception message becomes: `run() takes no keyword arguments`, which makes Luigi unable to do anything.

I could push a quick&dirty patch, but I prefer asking before because you might prefer to get rid of this hack instead.

issue_title: TensorFlow is required when installing spotify-tensorflow
issue_body:
Right now, there's no tensorflow dependency listed in: https://github.com/spotify/spotify-tensorflow/blob/master/requirements.txt but if tensorflow is not installed, it fails because of this import https://github.com/spotify/spotify-tensorflow/blob/master/spotify_tensorflow/__init__.py#L22.

I think it is a good idea to not require tensorflow. One use case is using the luigi task definition, to run the tensorflow task with ml-engine. In that case we don't need to have tensorflow installed.

One possible solution is to move that import where it is actually needed.

issue_title: New DirecTV scanner not working with RVU units (i.e. DIRECTV C51-100)
issue_body:
Hi @cbulock 

The new DirecTV component works great with my main unit, but it does not seem to work with remote players (i.e. the Mini Genie remotes).

I have 3 DIRECTV C51-100 that are not detected.

The code appears to be checking for:
            "manufacturer": "DIRECTV",
            "deviceType": "urn:schemas-upnp-org:device:MediaServer:1"
while that actual device is

issue_body:
### Checklist

- [X] This is a bug report.
- [X] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description

When I attempt to open any stream, I receive the following traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 655, in _build_master

    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 657, in _build_master
    return cls._build_from_requirements(__requires__)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 670, in _build_from_requirements
    dists = ws.resolve(reqs, Environment())
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 855, in resolve
    raise DistributionNotFound(req, requirers)
pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound: The 'streamlink==0.1.0' distribution was not found and is required by the application

```
### Expected / Actual behavior

stream starts

### Reproduction steps / Explicit stream URLs to test

1. install via pip in python 3.6
2. ```streamlink --version```

### Environment details

Operating system and version: **Fedora 26**
Streamlink and Python version: **Python 3.6.1**

installed packages:

```
Looks like this is the script that is created @ ```usr/bin/streamlink```:
```
#!/usr/bin/python3
# EASY-INSTALL-ENTRY-SCRIPT: 'streamlink==0.1.0','console_scripts','streamlink'
__requires__ = 'streamlink==0.1.0'
import sys
from pkg_resources import load_entry_point

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(
        load_entry_point('streamlink==0.1.0', 'console_scripts', 'streamlink')()
    )
```

modifying the script to:
```
#!/usr/bin/python3

from pkg_resources import load_entry_point

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(
        load_entry_point('streamlink==0.7.0', 'console_scripts', 'streamlink')()
    )
```
resolves the problem, not sure about a long term solution.

proxy_server.py assumes a bare CONTENT_TYPE of ```application/x-www-form-urlencoded``` or ```application/json``` to correctly unpack POST data. My test applicatoin (Chrome Postman extension) sends POST requests with ```CONTENT_TYPE: application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8```, wich will fail the test at line 39. The correct way to check would be:

```if "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" in environ["CONTENT_TYPE"]:```

and at line 41:

```elif "application/json" in environ["CONTENT_TYPE"]:```

I can now correctly use the built-in oAuth 2.0 auth flow in Postman.

issue_title: Enabling SSE potentially slows down performance(?)
issue_body:
I'm experimenting with the Bullet physics engine version 2.82, and comparing scalar code implementation to SSE implementation (`BT_USE_SSE` and `BT_USE_SSE_IN_API` defined). My tests are being run on a Windows Haswell system with Visual Studio 2015 compiler. The scene consists of 1500 boxes falling from the sky in six tall columns. You can find an interactive HTML5 web build of the scene here: https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/40949268/emcc/2015-10-05-emunittest_0.4-PhysicsStressTest-Urho3D_1.4_e1.34.11-release-prof/12_PhysicsStressTest.html (please run in Firefox, preferably in 64-bit and in Nightly for best performance). Note that the web build is just for illustration purposes of what my scene looks like. I am not benchmarking the web build, but a native Windows 64-bit build.

The results I am getting are quite surprising: when I compile a 32-bit Windows executable with `BT_USE_SSE` and `BT_USE_SSE_IN_API` defined, I am getting roughly 10% *worse* performance compared to the scalar build. When comparing 64-bit builds, SSE is a bit faster than scalar, but only about 3% (tested one run, not much for statistical significance). I am profiling the hotspots with AMD CodeXL, and in each case, Bullet functions are consuming more than 90% of the total frametime, so the 3D engine driving the application should not be affecting the results. Looking at AMD CodeXL profiles confirms that the workload is saturated with Bullet simulation:

   - scalar: https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/40949268/dump/Urho3D_scalar.png
   - sse: https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/40949268/dump/Urho3D_sse.png

In the web build, that can be confirmed by hitting F2, and observing the times given by the internal profiler, "StepSimulation" is where Bullet physics is being driven.

I have fixed the timesteps I pass to `stepSimulation`, calling it at a constant rate of timeStep = 0.016667, maxSubSteps=0, internalTimeStep = 0.016667.

My question is whether there exists benchmarks of Bullet's sse performance posted somewhere? Are my results to be expected?

issue_title: Memory leak in getClosestPoints?
issue_body:
I am using pybullet to compute distances between a bunch of objects in a scene, but my RAM gets full very quickly... I tried to profile using `memory_profiler` and it seems that the memory allocated by `getClosestPoints` is never freed.

I wrote the following repro program:
```python
import pybullet as p
import pybullet_data
import os
import numpy as np

nr_ducks = 100
nr_steps = 1000

p.connect(p.DIRECT)

root = pybullet_data.getDataPath()

ducks = [p.loadURDF(os.path.join(root, 'duck_vhacd.urdf')) for i in range(nr_ducks)]

for duck in ducks:
    p.resetBasePositionAndOrientation(duck, 0.05*np.random.rand(3), [0, 0, 0, 1])

for i in range(nr_steps):
    distances = [p.getClosestPoints(ducks[0], duck, 0.01) for duck in ducks[1:]]
    del distances  # Should not even be needed

p.disconnect()
```
The resulting memory usage (`mprof run leak_pybullet.py`):
![leak_pybullet](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4352562/43060933-017bec26-8e8e-11e8-9827-8a0e09d75c0b.png)

issue_title: Handle Hawk signature with BATCH subrequests
issue_body:
Currently, BATCH operations need each request to specify their authentication. This is okay for BasicAuth but can be trickier for other authentication schemes. For instance, hawk needs each request to be signed with the content of the request, which adds complexity on the client side for almost no benefit.

One way to solve this and make it easier for clients would be to allow the specification of credentials in the BATCH request headers, and do not need to specify it during the sub-requests.

issue_title: Stop using print
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 281465844, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20refactoring', 'name': 'area: refactoring', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 200596079, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20ui%20/%20ux', 'name': 'area: ui / ux', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/Good%20Volunteer%20Task', 'name': 'Good Volunteer Task', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': False}, {'id': 560796203, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/sf-python-sprint', 'name': 'sf-python-sprint', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
In a few places in our code, we use `print` to bypass the line wrapping that `IDisplay` does. The problem with this is it requires us to duplicate code for things like `-q/--quiet`, making maintenance harder and increasing the chance for bugs.

Instead, we should either add an optional flag to the `notification` method to disable line wrapping or add a new method to `IDisplay` that doesn't format output.

issue_title: Use appropriate caution when handling configurations that have complex rewrite logic
issue_body:
If the user has a config file with complex redirects (eg conditional redirects), it's in general impossible for us to add HTTP -> HTTPS redirects safely.  It's even hard to create new vhosts safely, which is necessary in Apache (cf #3210 ).  Where possible, we should try to detect the sorts of conditional redirecting logic that create this danger ([here's an nginx example](http://redant.com.au/ruby-on-rails-devops/manage-ssl-redirection-in-nginx-using-maps-and-save-the-universe/); [here's an Apache example](http://serverfault.com/a/609348); but of course these will come in many flavours).

If we detect this sort of conditionality, we should at minimum warn the user before enabling any redirects, or possibly refuse to do it without some sort of override.

issue_title: unhelpful message (to stdout/stderr, as reported by cron via email): Encountered vhost ambiguity but unable to ask for user guidance in non-interactive mode
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
debian 9.1

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
certbot-auto (certbot version: 0.18.2)

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
(for my reference, this was apricot @ 2017-09-23 01:13:07,943)
`certbot-auto -q renew` (in a crontab)
```
Encountered vhost ambiguity but unable to ask for user guidance in non-interactive mode. Certbot may need vhosts to be explicitly labelled with ServerName or ServerAlias directives.
Falling back to default vhost *:443...
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
1. It doesn't indicate which domain is failing.
2. It should distinguish between "I found multiple vhosts that could be responsible for this domain", "I couldn't find any vhosts responsible for this domain", and "there used to be a vhost responsible for this domain, but it's missing/no longer covers this domain".
3. if a cron task produces no output, no email is sent. In general, a cron job should only produce output if an error has occurred.

certbot should probably conceptually queue console warnings like this one until it runs into an instance where it actually needed to ask and couldn't, as opposed to saying "well, I might, eventually, maybe, need some information, maybe, so why don't I bother the user needlessly now, because being eager is a really useful activity". In this case, no error occurred, and thus a warning like this is spurious and really frustrating.


## Here is a Certbot log showing the issue (if available):
###### Logs are stored in `/var/log/letsencrypt` by default. Feel free to redact domains, e-mail and IP addresses as you see fit.
```
2017-09-23 01:13:07,554:INFO:certbot.auth_handler:Performing the following challenges:
2017-09-23 01:13:07,554:INFO:certbot.auth_handler:tls-sni-01 challenge for example.com
2017-09-23 01:13:07,943:WARNING:certbot_apache.display_ops:Encountered vhost ambiguity but unable to ask for user guidance in non-interactive mode. Certbot may need vhosts to be explicitly labelled with ServerName or ServerAlias directives.
2017-09-23 01:13:07,943:WARNING:certbot_apache.tls_sni_01:Falling back to default vhost *:443...
```

## Here is the relevant nginx server block or Apache virtualhost for the domain I am configuring:
There isn't a vhost for this domain at this time... I'm not sure why. Mostly this domain is only used for email, and certbot is responsible for getting the certificate/renewing it so that dovecot/whatever can serve it.

PS. It did manage to renew the certificate, so, mostly this warning is incredibly disturbing, but not remotely helpful. As someone who gets a lot of email from cron/logwatch, this is an email I really could have done without.

issue_title: jQuery.parseJSON requires a valid JSON string: 204 response
issue_body:
Using flask as backend, it's impossible return anything but empty response along with status code 204. And [HTTP spec](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec10.html#sec10.2.5) explicitly says: 

> The 204 response MUST NOT include a message-body, and thus is always terminated by the first empty line after the header fields.

There is suggested solution retrieve data as text, but this seems like kludge and is ugly (IMHO). And I have number of endpoints in API with possible 204 response.

I believe jQuery / jQuery-Migrate should make exception for 204 status code and always return null for this case instead of trying parse response text.

Or I should report this in jQuery repo?

issue_title: Warn about unqoted attribute selectors
issue_body:
We've removed unofficial "support" for selectors with unquoted attribute values where it has to be quoted, like `a[href=#]` in jQuery 2.2.0/1.12.0. It turns out many people relied on that as we get a lot of bug reports about this change.

Migrate should warn about it.

issue_title: Problem getting Amalgamation to work for Android Studio
issue_body:
I had a lot of problems getting the latest release to compile in Amalgamation. I’m using Ubuntu 14.

Some files are missing e.g. src/ndarray/unary_function.cc

I see that they were moved/renamed in this commit https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/pull/1839/files in April. So commit 1839 broke the Amalgamation.

So I reverted to a commit from before April: Merge: d81adfa 45ee47c Author: Tianqi Chentqchen@users.noreply.github.com Date: Wed Mar 23 09:04:12 2016 -0700

Now I am able to compile it and generate jni_libmxnet_predict.so. I renamed to  libmxnet_predict.so to put it in the Android Studio Whatsthis app.

However then libmxnet_predict.so will not compile correctly in Android Studio.

```
                                                                Process: com.happen.it.make.whatsthis, PID: 27189
                                                                      java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: dlopen failed: cannot locate symbol "_ZN5mxnet21GraphStorageAllocatorC1EPNS_11StaticGraphERKSt6vectorIjSaIjEESt10shared_ptrINS_16GraphStoragePoolEE" referenced by "libmxnet_predict.so"...
```

I can see a lot of people had this problem (or similar):

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/34878870/ndk-cannot-locate-symbol-znwj

 https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/issues/1436 https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/issues/1613

it is necessary to compile this because the libmxnet_predict.so supplied in the Android WhatsThis app by Leliana only works for the models supplied with Mxnet. When I use the Caffe->Mxnet converter to generate an Mxnet model file, the Android WhatsThis app crashes with an error in the native code.

This happens even if I run https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/tree/master/tools/caffe_converter on VGG: the resultant model doesn’t work in Android Whatsthis. But no problems with iOS Whatsthis..

So I can suggest 2 alternatives:
1.  can someone please provide a version of libmxnet_predict.so that works on Android for any Caffe model converted via convert_model.py?  Then we need to update Whatsthis app.
2. does anybody know what is causing the java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError in the Android app when I compile  libmxnet_predict.so?

issue_title: using C++ api in windows10 to extract feature error
Fatal JVM Error due to Exception in CustomOpProp#inferTypeEntry
issue_body:
Hi,

## Description
I try to reimplement a simple version of a FullyConnected Layer. However, I ran into a problem that crashes the JVM, when I try to add the weight-argument.
This gist recreates the issue:
https://gist.github.com/tutnixzursache/99d335d1733ec0435c6a9e4068678a17

I use the 0.11.0 version, however, I think the problem exists in 0.12.0 as well.

The problem seems to be that the first time the inferTypeEntry method of the CustomOpProp class is called, the intputTypes parameter contains the value -1 for the weight-array entry (I assume because its type is not known at this point?). However, subsequently the method tries to transform this value into a DType for which no value corresponding to -1 exists:
https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/396943e22661f03867d103d134416541e7e4f2bb/scala-package/core/src/main/scala/ml/dmlc/mxnet/Operator.scala#L211

issue_title: Init bias term in every layer
Array out of bounds access
issue_body:
I reported this in #8133, but I think it got lost in my confusion about scalar values in mxnet. But I think this deserves a separate issue.

Other than numpy, mxnet doesn't support rank 0 arrays, but uses an empty tuples to represent an unknown shape. `mx.nd` still allows the creation of rank 0 arrays however. This leads to confusion during shape inference and allows out of bounds memory reads and writes:

```python
a = mx.sym.var('a')
b = mx.sym.var('b')

c = a + b

a_ = mx.nd.ones(()) # Has size 1, but bind assumes it has shape (5,)
b_ = mx.nd.ones(5)
func = c.bind(mx.cpu(), {'a': a_, 'b': b_})
func.forward()
func.outputs[0].asnumpy()
array([  2.00000000e+00,   2.52435490e-29,   8.84247875e+05,
         1.08446609e-19,  -1.92860745e-26], dtype=float32)
```

We can write to invalid memory (and crash the process or potentially get arbitrary code execution) by providing a gradient buffer with the wrong shape
```python
a = mx.sym.var('a')
grad = mx.sym.var('grad')

c = mx.sym.MakeLoss(a)

grad_ = mx.nd.ones(())
a_ = mx.nd.ones(500)
func = c.bind(mx.cpu(), {'a': a_}, args_grad={'a': grad_})
func.forward()
func.backward()
```

issue_title: RNN Example not supported for MXNet Scala

This model has not been updated since 2017/07, the RNN example for LstmBucketing is no longer runnable. 
Please contribute to RNN support on Scala for the current version. If you would like to run this example for now, please revert your MXNet version to `v0.12`. 

In summary, I suspect that some operator changes caused this issue.

- [ ] LstmBucketing
- [ ] TestCharRnn
- [x] TrainCharRNN

Please contact @lanking520 or @nswamy if you would like to dig in and fix this problem. The following is a way to reproduce the issues I found:

## LSTM Bucketing
### Setup
Download the required ptb file from here:
```Bash
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmlc/web-data/master/mxnet/ptb/ptb.train.txt
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmlc/web-data/master/mxnet/ptb/ptb.valid.txt
```
You can use this [script](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/scala-package/examples/scripts/rnn/run_lstm_bucketing.sh) to run the model 
or manually identify the file path by following [this](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/scala-package/examples/src/main/scala/org/apache/mxnetexamples/rnn/LstmBucketing.scala#L36-L48)

Please add the follows to [IO.scala](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/scala-package/core/src/main/scala/org/apache/mxnet/IO.scala#L358)
```Scala
if (shapes.toIndexedSeq(0)._2.length == 2) {
   shapes.map { case (k, s) => new DataDesc(k, s, layout = "NT") }.toIndexedSeq
}

issue_title: SSHOperator requires a Hook, not an ssh_conn_id
issue_body:
Dear Airflow Maintainers,

Before I tell you about my issue, let me describe my environment:

### Environment

* Version of Airflow (e.g. a release version, running your own fork, running off master -- provide a git log snippet):
* Airflow Version 1.7
* Scheduler with Local Executor against RDS Postgres

Now that you know a little about me, let me tell you about the issue I am having:

### Description of Issue

* What did you expect to happen? SSHOperator doesn't accept an ssh_conn_id, instead it wants a hook.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Any SSHOperator requires a hook by design.
